    LogParameters
    setUseParentLogging(Boolean useParentLogging)
    If true, statements will appear in the standard WebLogic log files.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Field Details

    • useParentLogging

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Write to parent logger, i.e. the WebLogic engine log file. Default: false") protected Boolean useParentLogging

    • directory

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Location of log files. Supports environment and servlet context variables. Default: ./servers/${weblogic.Name}/logs/vorpal") protected String directory

    • filename

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Name of the log file. Supports environment and servlet context variables. Default: ${sip.application.name}.%g.log") protected String filename

    • fileSize

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Maximum file size written in human readable form. Default: 100MiB") protected String fileSize

    • fileCount

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Maximum number of log files. Default: 25") protected Integer fileCount

    • appendFile

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Continue to use the same log file after reboot. Default: true") protected Boolean appendFile

    • loggingLevel

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Logging level. Levels include: OFF, SEVERE, WARNING, INFO, CONFIG, FINE, FINER, FINEST, ALL. Default: FINE") protected LogParameters.LoggingLevel loggingLevel

    • sequenceDiagramLoggingLevel

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Level at which sequence diagrams will be logged. Default: FINE") protected LogParameters.LoggingLevel sequenceDiagramLoggingLevel

    • configurationLoggingLevel

      @JsonPropertyDescription("Level at which configuration changes will be logged. Default: FINE") protected LogParameters.LoggingLevel configurationLoggingLevel

  • Method Details

    • getLoggingLevel

      public LogParameters.LoggingLevel getLoggingLevel()

    • getUseParentLogging

      public Boolean getUseParentLogging()
      If true, statements will appear in the standard WebLogic log files. If false, statements will appear in custom application specific log files.
      Returns:
      the useParentLogging

    • setUseParentLogging

      public LogParameters setUseParentLogging(Boolean useParentLogging)
      If true, statements will appear in the standard WebLogic log files. If false, statements will appear in custom application specific log files.
      Parameters:
      useParentLogging - the useParentLogging value
      Returns:
      instance of self
